title: POST /books dead over HTTP — handler init/2 calls nonexistent cowboy_req:match_params/1
evidence: src/book_api_handler.erl:9 `cowboy_req:match_params(Req0)`. Verified against _build/default/lib/cowboy/src/cowboy_req.erl (cowboy 2.10.0): 0 occurrences of match_params; exports only binding/2,3, bindings/1, match_qs/2, parse_qs/1. init/2 runs on every /books request and raises undef before dispatching. book_api_db:create_book/1 works (tests call it directly with atom keys), but the HTTP create route is dead. Exact unfixed FEEDBACK defect.
suggestion: Replace cowboy_req:match_params(Req0) with cowboy_req:binding(id, Req0, undefined) and branch on undefined.

title: GET /books dead over HTTP — same match_params/1 undef crash
evidence: src/book_api_handler.erl:9 — init/2 unconditionally calls cowboy_req:match_params/1 before reaching handle_books_collection_request(<<"GET">>, _) at line 38, so list-all crashes with undef on every request. DB get_all_books/0 works but is never reached over HTTP.
suggestion: Fix the binding extraction in init/2 (see R1); the GET collection clause itself is otherwise correct.

title: ?author= filter broken three ways: nonexistent cowboy_req:query_param/2, unreachable, and type-mismatched
evidence: src/book_api_handler.erl:39 `cowboy_req:query_param(<<"author">>, Req)` — verified absent from cowboy 2.10.0 cowboy_req.erl (0 occurrences; use match_qs/2 or parse_qs/1). Unreachable anyway due to the match_params undef at line 9. Also line 41 binary_to_list(AuthorVal) yields a string(list) then line 61 `maps:get(author, Book) =:= AuthorName` compares it to the binary the DB returns, so the guard never matches. Confirms first evaluator on all points.
suggestion: Use cowboy_req:match_qs([{author, [], undefined}], Req) and compare against a binary, keeping author as a binary throughout.

title: GET /books/{id} dead over HTTP — same match_params/1 undef crash
evidence: src/book_api_handler.erl:9 crashes with undef before handle_book_request(<<"GET">>, Id, _) at line 76 runs. book_api_db:get_book_by_id/1 (with 404 on empty) is correct but unreachable over HTTP.

title: PUT /books/{id} dead over HTTP — match_params/1 undef plus jiffy decode defect
evidence: src/book_api_handler.erl:9 crashes with undef before the PUT clause at line 90. Even if reached, line 93 `jiffy:decode(Body)` without return_maps returns an ejson tuple `{[...]}` so the is_map guard at line 98 never matches -> function_clause. DB update_book/2 is correct but unreachable.
suggestion: Fix init/2 (R1) and pass [return_maps] to jiffy:decode with binary-key handling (see R8).

title: DELETE /books/{id} dead over HTTP — same match_params/1 undef crash
evidence: src/book_api_handler.erl:9 crashes with undef before handle_book_request(<<"DELETE">>, Id, _) at line 116 runs. DB delete_book/1 (204 / 404) is correct but unreachable over HTTP.

title: JSON bodies never decoded to maps — jiffy:decode/1 without return_maps; is_map guard never matches; atom vs binary key mismatch in DB
evidence: src/book_api_handler.erl:19 (POST) and :93 (PUT) call `jiffy:decode(Body)` with no [return_maps] option, so jiffy returns ejson `{[{<<"title">>,V},...]}` (a tuple, not a map). The `Decoded when is_map(Decoded)` guards at lines 24/98 never match, and the `{error, Reason}` clauses at 20/94 don't match either (jiffy throws on bad JSON) -> function_clause crash. Additionally src/book_api_db.erl:134,172 `maps:get(title, Attrs)`/`maps:get(author, Attrs)` use atom keys, so even with return_maps the binary JSON keys would badkey. (First evaluator cited db line 113; the actual atom-key sites are 134/172 — line off, defect real.) Only /health returns JSON successfully over HTTP.
suggestion: Decode with jiffy:decode(Body, [return_maps]) and read binary keys (maps:get(<<"title">>, Attrs)), or normalize keys to atoms before calling the DB.

title: Input validation exists in DB layer but is unreachable over HTTP
evidence: src/book_api_db.erl:248 validate_book/1 rejects missing title (title_required) / author (author_required) and create_book calls it at line 139, mapping to a 400 in the handler. But the HTTP create path is dead (R1), so 'creating without title/author is rejected (400)' cannot be exercised over HTTP.
suggestion: Restore the POST route (R1/R8); the validation logic itself is sound.

title: Test suite partially failing — test_coverage=0.5454
evidence: scores.json test_coverage=0.5454545 (~6 of 11 assertions pass). Tests in test/book_api_integration_tests.erl and test/book_api_db_tests.erl call book_api_db directly with atom-keyed maps and never exercise book_api_handler, so they cannot catch the HTTP-layer defects. Failures likely stem from cross-test state on a persisted books.db (setup expects id=1). Build itself is clean (code_quality=1.0).
suggestion: Isolate the SQLite file per test (or DELETE all rows in setup) and add tests that drive the real cowboy routes so HTTP regressions are caught.
